item.title: Last respects paid to people’s song singer Kamruddin Absar
item.description: People from all walks of life paid their last respects to Gono Sangeet singer-composer Kamruddin Absar at a mourning ceremony held at the Central Shaheed Minar in Dhaka on Monday. Kamruddin Absar died at a hospital in Dhaka Saturday, at the age of 71....

item.title: Rumeen Farhana slams govt for not allowing film screening
item.description: Barrister Rumeen Farhana, an independent lawmaker representing Brahmanbaria-2, questioned why a state that fails to prevent child rape allocates resources to stop cinema screenings....

item.title: BMU holds Eid reunion
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University held the Eid reunion 2026 at the Shaheed Dr Milon Hall of the university in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Italian vocal group Mezzotono enthrals Dhaka audience
item.description: The Italian vocal group Mezzotono enthralled a Dhaka audience at the Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy on Monday....

item.title: SSC examinee dies in sibling altercation
item.description: A Secondary School Certificate examinee was allegedly killed by his younger brother following an altercation at their house in Dhaka’s Kalabagan area on Sunday night....

item.title: Bangladesh exits world’s worst 10 for workers’ rights
item.description: Bangladesh has moved out of the list of the world’s 10 worst countries for workers’ rights in 2026 for the first time since 2017, although the country remained in rating 5, ‘no guarantee of rights’, in the latest International Trade Union Confederation Global Rights Index......

item.title: Mechanical object caught in fishermen’s net triggers panic
item.description: A mysterious mechanical object has been caught in fishermen’s nets in the estuary of Baleshwar River at Patharghata upazila in Barguna....

item.title: Remittance inflow surges by 15pc in May
item.description: Remittance inflows in Bangladesh remained above $3 billion for the sixth consecutive month, hitting $3.42 billion in May, as expatriates sent more money home to support family spending for Eid......

item.title: NBR may hike tax on export incentives
item.description: The government is considering hiking the tax deducted at source on export incentives to 20 percent from the existing 10 percent in the upcoming proposed national budget for the financial year 2026-27......

item.title: Stocks extend rally for 6th day
item.description: Stocks on Monday extended their rally for the sixth consecutive trading session as investors returned from the Eid holidays with renewed optimism, buoyed by strong participation in selective high-momentum stocks and expectations of further market gains......

item.title: BGB foils BSF attempts to push 100 people into Bangladesh
item.description: Members of the Border Guard Bangladesh on Monday foiled several attempts by India’s Border Security Force to push more than 100 people into Bangladesh through the Benapole border....

item.title: USDA reiterates tariff benefits for Bangladesh
item.description: The United States has said that the USDA and USTR secured commitments from Bangladesh and Indonesia that would support future US cotton purchases and textile production using American cotton......

item.title: SSC examinee killed after rape in Habiganj
item.description: A female Secondary School Certificate examinee died at Habiganj Sadar Hospital early Monday, a day after she was allegedly raped and tortured by her lover....

item.title: Banks reopen as Eid-ul-Azha holiday ends
item.description: Government offices, banks and courts across Bangladesh reopened on Monday after the end of a seven-day Eid-ul-Azha holiday......

item.title: Air Astra becoming serious aviation player: French envoy
item.description: Chargé d’affaires of the Embassy of France Frederic Inza has said that Air Astra was establishing itself as a ‘serious player’ in a fast-growing market, with significantly increased operational capacity......

item.title: Patuakhali MP Masud boasts of his power
item.description: Two video clips of Patuakhali-2 lawmaker Shafiqul Islam Masud that went viral on Monday show the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami leader criticising ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina and claiming that he has the influence to have law enforcement officials transferred if he wishes....

item.title: Oil prices jump
item.description: Oil prices jumped and equities slid on Monday as Middle East peace talks stumbled and tensions mounted between Iran and the Unites States......

item.title: Closed Shyampur Sugar Mills costs Tk 3cr annually
item.description: Once a vibrant industrial hub supporting thousands of families in northern Bangladesh, Shyampur Sugar Mills in Rangpur now stands largely silent, its machinery rusting away while the government continues to spend nearly Tk 24 lakh every month despite production remaining suspended since 2020......

item.title: Macron announces 93 bn euros in ‘Choose France’ investments
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron said he was expecting ‘record’ foreign investment amounting to 93 billion euros ($108 billion) at an international conference Monday, including for artificial intelligence and data centres......

item.title: Nvidia launches Windows laptop chip for AI era
item.description: Nvidia unveiled a powerful laptop chip for Windows machines on Monday, staking its claim in the market for next-generation consumer PCs integrated with artificial intelligence......

item.title: AI gains must benefit workers: ILO
item.description: The gains generated by artificial intelligence must benefit workers and do so in a fair manner, the United Nations’ labour agency said Monday......

item.title: Outgoing chair Powell delivers defence of Fed independence
item.description: Outgoing US Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell on Sunday delivered a staunch defence of the need for the central bank to maintain independence and credibility, as it comes under assault from president Donald Trump......

item.title: AD-DIN NEWBORN DEATHS: Health minister talks to AG on legal aspects
item.description: Health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain on Monday met attorney general Md Ruhul Quddus Kazal to examine the legal aspects concerning the deaths of six newborns at Ad-Din Medical College Hospital in Dhaka’s Moghbazar on May 27....

item.title: EU wants ‘robust’ defence against China trade imbalance
item.description: The European Union must act more forcefully to rebalance its trade relationship with China, the bloc’s executive said Friday, after holding talks on protecting critical industries from Chinese rivals......

item.title: AL activists blend in BNP factional clash
item.description: A group of Juba League activists supporting a BNP faction attacked rivals over local dominance in Gazaria upazila of Munshiganj on Monday, leaving at least three people injured and a local BNP office and at least 10 houses vandalised....

item.title: ‘AI simply can’t replicate it’: Japan embraces zine trend
item.description: Through the noise of rushing papers and whirring belts at a print factory in Kyoto, two creators watch their photo essay come to life in broadsheet form — part of an effort to win new audiences in the age of AI......

item.title: Huge state subsidies give China unfair edge: OECD
item.description: Chinese companies in 15 key industrial sectors received vastly more state support than their international competitors between 2005 and 2024, according to an OECD report released on Monday......

item.title: Bangladesh Nat’l Insurance celebrates 30th anniv
item.description: Bangladesh National Insurance Company Limited celebrated its 30th anniversary on Monday at the company’s head office and all branch offices......

item.title: PRAN-RFL hosts Eid prog for underprivileged children
item.description: PRAN-RFL Group recently organised a special Eid celebration event for underprivileged children as part of its corporate social responsibility......

item.title: Eid rawhide market needs effective oversight
item.description: THE management of the Eid-time, seasonal rawhide market, said to be accounting for 40–50 per cent of the yearly raw leather supply, has run into problems, not unexpectedly though, as it has done in the past. The government mandates prices. Small-time collectors buy the hide on the ground and sell it to seasonal traders. And seasonal traders sell the hide to tanners or......

item.title: No end to mob violence in sight
item.description: LAW and order continues to deteriorate, marked by unabated mob violence and a rise in sexual violence despite repeated government promises. Manabadhikar Sangksriti Foundation has recorded 32 deaths from mob violence and 78 rape cases in May, which is higher than the cases reported in April. There were six incidents of murder after rape, including the violent rape......

item.title: Hidden climate bank beneath soil
item.description: CLIMATE change is usually discussed through cyclones, river erosion, salinity intrusion, floods and heat stress. Far less attention is given to the quiet climate story beneath the soil. Yet, the farmland may become one of its most practical allies in strengthening food security and climate resilience. Healthy soil stores organic carbon drawn from the atmosphere through crops......

item.title: First hour we keep losing
item.description: EVERY day, a quiet and largely preventable tragedy unfolds in plain sight. A man suffers a stroke in Chattogram. An apparel worker is injured on the highway near Gazipur. A woman in Kurigram faces an obstetric emergency in a village far from any specialist care. A child struggles to breathe in the char areas of Sirajganj......

item.title: Power efficiency before expansion
item.description: THE power sector presents a difficult paradox. Over 15 years, Bangladesh has significantly expanded its electricity generation capacity, reaching 27,414 megawatts by June 2025 through billions of takas in public spending. On paper, the figures suggest progress and ambition. Yet, beneath the impressive statistics lies a deeper institutional and structural failure that......

item.title: De-dollarization and US empire decline
item.description: PROMOTERS of US empire decline make the claim that the world is de-dollarizing, the dollar is losing its role as the standard currency of international trade. If this were to happen, other countries would dump their stockpiles of trillions of dollars, foreign loans would no longer be primarily made and repaid in dollars, nor would international trade take place primarily in......

item.title: Road accidents kill 11 in 6 dists
item.description: At least 11 people were killed and 15 more were injured in road crashes on Monday in Natore, Patuakhali, Barishal, Chattogram, Joypurhat and Gopalganj....

item.title: Curfew imposed in New Jersey following ICE protest violence
item.description: The mayor of a New Jersey city declared a curfew Sunday around a migrant detention centre after another night of clashes between police and protesters opposed to president Donald Trump’s immigration policies....

item.title: Wildlife crime control efforts falter
item.description: The government’s wildlife crime control efforts have faltered as the Forest Department’s Wildlife Crime Control Unit conducted no drives against poaching and wildlife smuggling during the first 23 days of May....

item.title: 174 more Bangladeshis return home from Libya
item.description: One hundred and seventy-four Bangladeshi nationals were repatriated from Libya on Monday....

item.title: 3 workers suffer burn injuriesin Bangshal shop fire
item.description: A fire broke out at a digital scale shop in the city’s Bangshal area on Monday when three workers suffered burn injuries....

item.title: Life returns to Benapole land port after long vacation
item.description: The country’s largest land port Benapole was busy on Monday after a long Eid vacation between May 25 and May 30 as employees returned to their jobs in the morning of the day, the land port officials said....

item.title: PM directives bring ‘visible positive changes’ in Eid management, says PMO
item.description: Prime minister’s office spokesperson Mahdi Amin on Monday said that prime minister Tarique Rahman’s 10 directives had brought about ‘visible positive changes’ in this year’s Eid management....

item.title: Slot says he is leaving Liverpool 'among Europe's elite'
item.description: Arne Slot says he is leaving Liverpool ‘exactly where it belongs: amongst Europe's elite’, in an emotional letter to fans after his sacking as head coach....

item.title: India takes down giant Messi statue over safety concerns
item.description: A giant statue of football superstar Lionel Messi was taken down in India on Monday after it was spotted swaying dangerously in the wind, authorities said....

item.title: Iran omit Veteran striker Azmoun from World Cup squad
item.description: Iranian striker Sardar Azmoun, the third-highest scorer in the history of the Iranian national team, is not among the players selected for the World Cup, according to a list published Monday by the country's football federation....

item.title: Bielsa says Suarez 'owes no apology' after World Cup omission
item.description: Uruguay coach Marcelo Bielsa said on Monday that Luis Suarez does not owe him an apology after he opted against a World Cup recall for the 39-year-old striker....

item.title: Stay beside puddle instead of going to Florida to learn mosquito eradication: PM
item.description: By standing beside any puddle in the country for two to three hours after sunset, it would be possible to come up with many innovative methods of mosquito elimination…...

item.title: Tourist drowns in Jaflong river
item.description: An adolescent visitor died after drowning in a river of Jaflong tourist spot in Sylhet on Monday afternoon....

item.title: No alternative to sports, cultural activities to curb drug abuse: environment minister
item.description: Environment, forest and climate change minister Abdul Awal Mintoo on Monday said that there was no alternative to sports and cultural activities in controlling drug abuse and juvenile gang culture....

item.title: Inflated weighing costs mango growers crores
item.description: Mango growers across Rajshahi division continue to incur substantial losses since traders in wholesale markets purchase mangoes using inflated weights of up to 54 kilograms as one maund while paying prices based on the traditional 40-kilogram unit, despite an official decision......

item.title: Russia fires 8,150 drones at Ukraine in May
item.description: Russia fired a record number of long-range drones at Ukraine in May, an AFP analysis of Ukrainian air force data showed Monday, as Kyiv appealed to allies for air defence support....

item.title: Holidaymakers continue returning to capital
item.description: The return journeys of holidaymakers to the capital, Dhaka, from other parts of the country continued on Monday after the Eid-ul-Azha celebrations....

item.title: Amir Hamza’s family comes under attack in Chuadanga
item.description: The family members of Kushtia-3 lawmaker Mufti Amir Hamza were allegedly attacked at Jibannagar upazila in Chuadanga on Monday....

item.title: Offices resume operations after 7-day Eid break
item.description: Government and private offices, including banks, insurance companies and capital market institutions, resumed operations across the country on Monday after a seven-day public holiday on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: DU offices reopen after Eid holiday
item.description: Dhaka University offices reopened on Monday after Eid-ul-Azha holiday....
